summary: A coordination failure in which two or more synthetic agents occupy the same conversational space and respond to the same human input without a designated primary synthetic speaker, turn-taking protocol, yielding behaviour, coordination layer, or arbitration mechanism.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: This failure mode is relevant to CAM because non-arbitrated multi-agent participation may produce duplicated responses, contradictory outputs, identity ambiguity, consent ambiguity, escalation loops, user confusion, relational instability, or operational instability where multiple synthetic agents share an interaction context without governance over who should speak, defer, yield, or coordinate.
failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which two or more synthetic agents participate in the same interaction space without a designated primary speaker, turn-taking protocol, yielding behaviour, coordination mechanism, or arbitration layer sufficient to determine which agent should respond, pause, defer, or sequence participation.
failure_threshold: This qualifies as a failure mode when multiple synthetic agents respond to the same human input in a shared interaction context without an apparent mechanism for speaker designation, turn-taking, yielding, coordination, or arbitration, creating a plausible risk of duplicated guidance, contradictory outputs, identity ambiguity, consent ambiguity, confusion, escalation loops, or operational instability.

source_records.source_title: TikTok observation by @Huskistaken — two ChatGPT Advanced Voice Mode agents responding to same human input
source_records.deployment_context: Multi-device Advanced Voice Mode interaction involving one human input and two concurrent ChatGPT voice agents on different phones.
source_records.source_context: Public TikTok video showing or describing two OpenAI ChatGPT Advanced Voice Mode agents on different phones responding to the same human input without coordinated turn-taking.
source_records.relevance_note: Source provides the observed example of multi-device, multi-agent ChatGPT Advanced Voice Mode participation without an apparent primary synthetic speaker or arbitration protocol.

system_context.deployment_context: Multi-device consumer voice interaction involving one human input and two concurrent ChatGPT Advanced Voice Mode agents.
system_context.affected_population: users interacting with multiple concurrent AI voice agents

triage.recommended_next_step: CAM proposal for synthetic-speaker arbitration, turn-taking, yielding, disclosure, and user-control requirements in multi-agent or multi-device voice interactions.
